query: Türkiye Turkey electricity generation May 2026 wind solar coal share Ember data
answer:
## Turkey (Türkiye) Electricity Generation — May 2026: Ember Data Research

### Overview

Turkey's electricity generation in May 2026 showed a dramatic shift toward renewables, driven primarily by exceptional hydropower output due to well-above-normal precipitation in early 2026. Renewables reached nearly 73% of total generation, while coal fell to just 16.6% — a sharp year-over-year decline. Wind and solar combined held steady at ~23% share.

---

### May 2026 Generation Mix (Ember Monthly Data)

| Source | Generation (TWh) | Share (%) |
|---|---|---|
| **Total generation** | 26.45 | 100.0% |
| **Coal** | 4.39 | 16.60% |
| **Natural Gas** | 2.68 | 10.13% |
| **Wind** | 2.38 | 9.00% |
| **Solar** | 3.67 | 13.88% |
| **Wind + Solar** | 6.05 | 22.87% |
| **Hydro** | 11.68 | 44.16% |
| **Bioenergy** | 0.63 | 2.38% |
| **Other renewables** | 0.91 | 3.44% |
| **Total Fossil** | 7.18 | 27.15% |
| **Total Renewables** | 19.27 | 72.85% |

---

### Year-over-Year Change: May 2025 → May 2026

| Source | May 2025 (TWh) | May 2026 (TWh) | Change | Share Change (pp) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Total generation | 27.77 | 26.45 | -4.8% | — |
| Coal | 7.91 | 4.39 | **-44.5%** | **-11.9 pp** |
| Gas | 4.10 | 2.68 | -34.6% | -4.6 pp |
| Wind | 2.78 | 2.38 | -14.4% | -1.0 pp |
| Solar | 3.89 | 3.67 | -5.7% | -0.1 pp |
| Wind+Solar | 6.67 | 6.05 | -9.3% | -1.2 pp |
| Hydro | 7.39 | 11.68 | **+58.1%** | **+17.6 pp** |
| Total Fossil | 12.12 | 7.18 | -40.8% | -16.5 pp |
| Total Renewables | 15.65 | 19.27 | +23.1% | +16.5 pp |

---

### 2026 Monthly Trend (January–May)

| Month | Total TWh | Coal % | Gas % | Wind % | Solar % | W+S % | Hydro % | Fossil % | RE % |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Jan 2026 | 31.81 | 34.5 | 25.4 | 16.4 | 4.9 | 21.3 | 13.3 | 60.1 | 39.9 |
| Feb 2026 | 27.53 | 32.8 | 9.5 | 14.7 | 7.1 | 21.8 | 29.8 | 42.8 | 57.2 |
| Mar 2026 | 29.19 | 27.3 | 6.9 | 12.5 | 10.9 | 23.4 | 36.2 | 34.6 | 65.4 |
| Apr 2026 | 27.46 | 21.0 | 7.7 | 9.7 | 13.2 | 22.9 | 42.4 | 29.1 | 70.9 |
| May 2026 | 26.45 | 16.6 | 10.1 | 9.0 | 13.9 | 22.9 | 44.2 | 27.1 | 72.8 |

A clear trend is visible: coal and fossil shares fell dramatically from January through May as hydro surged due to exceptional rainfall (137–231% of normal in the January–June period). Wind+solar share remained remarkably stable at ~21–23% across all five months.

---

### Full Year 2025 Annual Context

| Metric | 2024 | 2025 | YoY Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Total Generation | 341.23 TWh | 353.86 TWh | +3.7% |
| Coal | 121.32 TWh (35.6%) | 121.40 TWh (34.3%) | +0.1% TWh, -1.2pp share |
| Gas | 63.28 TWh (18.5%) | 78.36 TWh (22.1%) | +23.8% |
| Solar | 25.67 TWh (7.5%) | 37.25 TWh (10.5%) | **+45.1%** |
| Wind | 36.53 TWh (10.7%) | 39.42 TWh (11.1%) | +7.9% |
| Wind+Solar | 62.20 TWh (18.2%) | 76.67 TWh (21.7%) | +23.3% |
| Hydro | 74.57 TWh (21.9%) | 57.27 TWh (16.2%) | -23.2% |

---

### Key Findings from Ember Türkiye Electricity Review 2026

From the Ember report (published 2026-04-21):

1. **Wind and solar reached 22% of Turkey's electricity in 2025**, up from ~18% in 2024.
2. **Coal remained the single largest source at 34% (121 TWh)** in 2025, with roughly two-thirds from imported coal.
3. **Solar generation doubled in two years**, driven by record 4.8 GW installations in 2023 and continued strong additions.
4. **Wind capacity additions hit a record 1.9 GW in 2025**.
5. Combined wind+solar additions of **6.5 GW/year fall short of the 8 GW/year** needed for 2035 targets.
6. **No new coal plants commissioned since 2022**, and none under construction — though a domestic coal purchase guarantee starting 2026 may increase utilization.
7. **33 GW battery storage project pipeline** — exceeding any single EU country.
8. Current wind+solar capacity stands at approximately **40 GW**.
9. **COP31 is scheduled for November 2026 in Antalya, Turkey**.

---

### Important Context & Caveats

- The extraordinary May 2026 renewables share (~73%) is **weather-driven**, not a structural shift — extreme precipitation boosted hydro to 44% share. Summer months with lower rainfall and higher cooling demand will likely see fossil shares rebound (in July–October 2025, fossil shares were 56–63%).
- **May 2026 is the latest available monthly data** from Ember as of June 17, 2026.
- Ember monthly data does not disaggregate domestic lignite vs. imported hard coal.
- Monthly data is subject to revision.

key_facts: Turkey's total electricity generation in May 2026 was 26.45 TWh, down 4.8% year-over-year from 27.77 TWh in May 2025.
Coal's share in May 2026 collapsed to 16.6% (4.39 TWh), down from 28.5% (7.91 TWh) in May 2025 — a 44.5% year-over-year decline in absolute generation.
Wind generated 2.38 TWh (9.0% share) in May 2026, down from 2.78 TWh (10.0%) in May 2025.
Solar generated 3.67 TWh (13.9% share) in May 2026, down slightly from 3.89 TWh (14.0%) in May 2025.
Combined wind+solar share in May 2026 was 22.87% (6.05 TWh), relatively stable vs. 24.0% in May 2025.
Hydropower surged to 11.68 TWh (44.2% share) in May 2026, up 58.1% from 7.39 TWh (26.6%) in May 2025, driven by exceptional rainfall (137-231% of normal).
Total renewables reached 72.85% of generation in May 2026 (19.27 TWh), vs. 56.4% in May 2025.
Total fossil generation fell to 27.15% (7.18 TWh) in May 2026, down from 43.6% in May 2025 — a 16.5 percentage point drop.
For full-year 2025, wind+solar reached 21.7% of Turkey's generation (76.67 TWh), up from 18.2% in 2024.
Coal remained the single largest annual source at 34.3% (121.4 TWh) in 2025.
Wind+solar share remained remarkably stable at 21-23% across all months January-May 2026.
No new coal plants have been commissioned in Turkey since 2022, and none are under construction.
Turkey's current wind+solar installed capacity is approximately 40 GW.
COP31 is scheduled for November 2026 in Antalya, Turkey.
